examples.title: Basic Data Types and Arithmetic Operators
examples.description: A program demonstrating fundamental C++ data types (int, float, double, char) and basic arithmetic operators.
examples.explanation: This example shows the fundamental data types in C++: int for integers, float and double for floating-point numbers, and char for characters. It demonstrates arithmetic operators including addition (+), subtraction (-), multiplication (*), division (/), and modulus (%). The division of two integers results in integer division. Increment (++) and decrement (--) operators are also shown.

examples.title: Comparison and Logical Operators with Type Conversion
examples.description: A program demonstrating comparison operators, logical operators, and type casting between different data types.
examples.explanation: This example demonstrates comparison operators (==, !=, >, <, >=, <=) which return boolean values. Logical operators (&&, ||, !) combine boolean expressions. The program also shows type casting: explicit casting using (double), and using static_cast<> for safer type conversion. Without casting, integer division loses decimal precision. Understanding type conversion is crucial for avoiding bugs in mathematical operations.
